name: Llama Guard (Meta)
pricing_notes: Open source model weights; self-hosted or via Groq/Together/Fireworks free tiers

auth.notes: If using hosted providers (Groq/Together): their API key; self-hosted: no auth

performance.notes: 8B model via Groq ~200ms; self-hosted GPU ~150ms; classification response only

agent_readiness.notes: Meta's open-source LLM safety classifier. Classifies conversations as safe/unsafe across hazard categories (violence, self-harm, sexual content, etc.). Available as Llama Guard 3 8B. Run via standard LLM inference (vLLM, Ollama, hosted APIs). No proprietary SDK.

gotchas: No official SDK — must use LLM inference providers or self-host
8B model requires GPU for fast inference
False positive rate varies by category and prompt style
Not designed for prompt injection detection (separate problem)
best_when: Open-source safety classification without paying per API call
Self-hosted LLM stacks needing integrated safety layer
Fine-tunable safety classifier for custom category definitions
Research on LLM safety and content classification
avoid_when: No GPU for self-hosting (cloud API add latency/cost)
Need turnkey safety solution with SLA (use Azure Content Safety)
Prompt injection is primary concern (wrong tool)
summary: Llama Guard is Meta's open-source safety classifier for LLM conversations, available as Llama Guard 3 8B. Classifies user/assistant turns as safe/unsafe across hazard categories. No proprietary SDK — run via vLLM, Ollama, or hosted providers (Groq, Together). Best for open-source stacks needing safety classification without per-call API fees. Fine-tunable for custom categories.
